executive_summary: Canada is experiencing an unprecedented ransomware surge with over 100 organizations compromised in the past 48 hours across all sectors. High-profile victims include Canada Goose (retail), National Money Mart (financial services with database leaked), Eastern Townships School Board (education), Peterborough Public Health (public sector), and Municipality of North Perth. The Qilin ransomware group leads the campaign with 20+ victims, followed by Play, Safepay, and Clop gangs. Critical infrastructure including healthcare, telecommunications, public health, and municipal services have been breached, representing a national-level cyber emergency requiring immediate coordinated response.

items.targets: Service Star Freightways, Kuzco Lighting, Waterloo Information Systems, A Lococo Wholesale, Traffic Tech, Gradient Wind Engineering, Workers Health & Safety Centre, Bergmanis Preyra, Pre-Con Builders, Questica, Eastern Townships School Board, MG Chartered Professional Accountant, Watermark Beach Resort, CST Coal, BioPharma Services, NovAtel (Hexagon), Maheu&Maheu, Berts Electric, Capp Shupak - all Canadian organizations across transportation, technology, public sector, education, healthcare, and energy sectors
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service), T1589 (Gather Victim Identity Information), T1190 (Exploit Public-Facing Application)

canada_landscape.summary: Canada is facing a severe, coordinated ransomware crisis with over 100 organizations compromised across every major sector and province in the past 48 hours. Critical infrastructure including public health departments, municipalities, telecommunications networks, and a school board have been breached. No CCCS advisories were issued in the reporting period despite the national scale of this incident.
canada_landscape.retailers: Canada Goose (major apparel brand) compromised by Coinbase Cartel ransomware gang. Active Green + Ross (automotive retail chain) and Kids & Company (childcare services) compromised by Sinobi ransomware gang.
canada_landscape.financial: National Money Mart Company (financial services) compromised by Everest ransomware with full database exfiltration confirmed. Morison Insurance Brokers compromised by Sinobi. Trucash (financial technology) compromised by Lynx. Multiple accounting firms and financial service providers also affected including Pewarchuk CPA, MG Chartered Professional Accountant, and Axion50plus.

report_2w.executive_summary: CISA warns that Oracle WebLogic Server vulnerabilities (CVE-2024-21182) are being actively exploited in the wild, targeting versions 12.2.1.4.0 and 14.1.1.0.0. The Canadian Centre for Cyber Security (CCCS) issued Alert AL26-013 regarding a security incident impacting GitHub internal repositories. ShinyHunters ransomware group leaked 4.9 million Charter Communications customer records following a social engineering attack on an employee's Microsoft account. Red Hat and npm ecosystems face active supply-chain attacks with credential-stealing campaigns embedding malicious packages. A new HTTP/2 Bomb vulnerability enables remote denial-of-service attacks against NGINX, Apache, IIS, Envoy, and Cloudflare.

items.description: Oracle WebLogic Server vulnerability enabling remote exploitation
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: High

items.description: Mirasvit Full Page Cache Warmer Deserialization of Untrusted Data Vulnerability

items.description: Android zero-day vulnerability already exploited in targeted attacks
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: Medium

items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: Medium, Integrity: High, Availability: Low

items.description: VMware vCenter denial of service vulnerability
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: None, Integrity: None, Availability: High

canada_landscape.summary: The Canadian Centre for Cyber Security (CCCS) issued Alert AL26-013 on May 29, 2026, regarding a security incident impacting GitHub internal repositories. Citizen Lab has urged Canada to withdraw parts of Bill C-22 over privacy concerns. No direct incidents involving major Canadian retailers or financial institutions were identified in this reporting period.
